svg.text: ATTACHMENT D // PATTERN OF ACTIVITY // SABLE RIDGE

g.text: FOLKLORE / pre-1900
Trappers avoid the ridge; compasses spin. Locals
say it "answers whistles." An 1888 diary records a
hunting party that "followed a voice" and lost two.
A USGS survey team abandons a ridge cabin after
"equipment fouling" and one member's breakdown.
Site automated. High-power radar runs unmanned.
Operators log a faint nightly carrier: "the Sable hum."
~14 MONTHS AGO
First PHANTOM RETURNS in self-diagnostics: contacts
with no transponder, no vector, gone between sweeps.
~3 MONTHS AGO (autumn)
Livestock and pets die "frozen through," no wounds —
one dog solid in a heated barn. Radio "bleed" grows;
operators say the static answers, and twice "said a name."
~10 WEEKS AGO (October)
The Petrie brothers vanish near Cold Cauldron Pond.
Truck found running, radio tuned to static. Search fails.
~6 WEEKS AGO
FAA flags escalating phantom returns; schedules service.
A teen livestreams "breathing static" from the road,
then deletes it and will not discuss it.
~2 WEEKS AGO
Dr. Rostova arrives to service the array. Calls home
turn strange: it is "receiving more than it transmits."
Anomalous cold settles around the dome.
~4 DAYS AGO
Deputy Vance checks the road after a trucker's CB
"said his kids' names." He grows agitated; calls the
site again and again.
~18 HOURS AGO
CORRUPTED DATA LOOP transmits. Site goes dark.
No remote reboot. Failsafe alerts trip.
~6 HOURS AGO — NOW
Vance drives up alone. No check-in. You are activated.
► The curve is steepening. Whatever it is, it is
almost finished learning how to reach through.
